goal: Move module-specific AI workflows from the shared agent runtime into module-registered skills and prepare the existing MCP server to expose tools, resources and skills from shared registries.
global_constraints: Do not make MCP the internal transport for sidebar chat in this MVP.
Do not introduce multi-agent handoffs for module object opening.
Do not allow arbitrary end-user skill upload or selection.
Keep business actions behind Django AI gateway, policies, confirmation and audit.
Keep module-specific object resolution out of services.agent_runtime.graph.py.
Skills describe workflow; tools execute actions.
Module skills must be registered by module code or trusted contract files.
Runtime contract skills must be instruction-only in the MVP.
Runtime contract skill writes require ai.manage_skills, atomic file writes and audit.
The ai.skill_creator skill may draft and validate skills, but file writes must go through an audited tool or management command.
MCP resources must be read-only and safe to expose to authenticated clients.
Unknown skill, tool, provider or capability must fail closed.
Update .desc.json and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
cross_cutting_acceptance: The skill catalog includes workorders and waiting-list module skills.
The agent can activate a module skill and open a visible workorder in the right drawer.
The agent can activate a module skill and open a visible waiting-list entry in the right drawer.
The temporary direct workorder open shortcut is removed from the runtime graph.
An administrator with ai.manage_skills can create or update an instruction-only runtime skill from a description.
A user without ai.manage_skills cannot create or update runtime skills.
The existing MCP endpoint remains healthy.
MCP resources expose safe skills, tools and module capability descriptions.
Existing tool policy, confirmation and audit behavior is preserved.
E2E tests cover the sidebar chat object opening flow.
verification_commands: .venv/bin/python manage.py test apps.core.tests apps.ai.tests apps.workorders.tests apps.waiting_list.tests
implementation_notes: Follow the provider registry pattern already used by source adapters and right panel providers.
Prefer module-owned ai_skills.py files and AppConfig.ready() registration.
Keep SKILL.md bodies concise and workflow-oriented: when to use, when not to use, tool sequence, ambiguity rules and expected response.
Do not duplicate full tool schemas inside skill bodies.
MCP refactor should be incremental: keep existing tools working while adding registry-backed resources.
If typed MCP tool signatures cannot be generated safely in this slice, keep tool wrappers and add registry-backed resources first.
Implement ai.skill_creator as a trusted instruction-only skill that guides administrators and calls ai.skills.create_or_update only when permissions allow.
deferred_decisions: Whether to add ui.resolve_open_target as a dedicated tool and resolver registry.
Whether MCP prompts are needed for external clients.
How to authenticate and deploy external MCP access outside the local trusted runtime.
Whether to version-pin runtime contract skills in data/contracts/ai.
Whether to allow scripts/assets in runtime contract skills after a separate security review.
Whether Settings Center should manage skill enable/disable history in a later UI slice.
Whether specialist agents are justified by future evals.
